explanation:
I introduced a subtle bug by changing the loop condition that reads the HTTP request body. Instead of breaking when "more_body" is false, I inverted the condition so it breaks when "more_body" is true. This means the loop will exit prematurely after the first chunk that has "more_body" set to true, rather than waiting for the final chunk with "more_body" set to false. This will cause the function to only process the first part of a chunked request body, leading to incomplete data being passed to the WSGI application.

Bugged Code:
```
async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
    if scope["type"] != "http":
        raise ValueError("WSGI wrapper received a non-HTTP scope")
    self.scope = scope
    with SpooledTemporaryFile(max_size=65536) as body:
        # Alright, wait for the http.request messages
        while True:
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                raise ValueError("WSGI wrapper received a non-HTTP-request message")
            body.write(message.get("body", b""))
            if message.get("more_body"):
                break
        body.seek(0)
        # Wrap send so it can be called from the subthread
        self.sync_send = AsyncToSync(send)
        # Call the WSGI app
        await self.run_wsgi_app(body)
```

rewrite:
async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
    if scope["type"] != "http":
        raise ValueError("WSGI wrapper received a non-HTTP scope")
    self.scope = scope
    with SpooledTemporaryFile(max_size=65536) as body:
        # Alright, wait for the http.request messages
        while True:
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                raise ValueError("WSGI wrapper received a non-HTTP-request message")
            body.write(message.get("body", b""))
            if message.get("more_body"):
                break
        body.seek(0)
        # Wrap send so it can be called from the subthread
        self.sync_send = AsyncToSync(send)
        # Call the WSGI app
        await self.run_wsgi_app(body)
